skill.name: imagegen
skill.description: Generate AI images through the verging.ai API using DALL-E 3 or GPT-Image-1 models.
skill.summary: Create text-to-image assets with verging.ai image generation endpoints.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown examples and model documentation. Network and API-key findings are true positives because the skill sends prompts and authenticated requests to verging.ai, which is legitimate for the feature but requires user trust in a third-party service. No evidence found of prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Receives Image Prompts and API Keys
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network and credential-context findings. The skill instructs users to authenticate to verging.ai and send image prompts to a verging.ai API endpoint, which is the core feature but exposes prompts and API credentials to a third-party proxy.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly document verging.ai URLs and Authorization headers with an API key placeholder. The behavior appears intentional and necessary for the skill, but it still creates third-party data exposure.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for Ruby/shell backtick execution. The cited lines are Markdown code fences, inline code spans, or copyable setup examples; no executable script file is present in the audited file list.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of README.md and SKILL.md shows documentation text only. The static analyzer matched Markdown backticks and fenced shell snippets rather than runnable package code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for weak cryptographic algorithm findings. The cited lines contain model descriptions, parameter tables, or documentation headings, and no cryptographic API or hashing operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain plain documentation, not calls to MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithms. No evidence found for cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick visual assets without leaving their AI coding workflow. This skill documents how to call verging.ai image generation endpoints and retrieve CDN-hosted image URLs.
actual_capabilities: Documents authenticated calls to the verging.ai image generation API.
Supports DALL-E 3 and GPT-Image-1 model selection.
Explains prompt, model, size, quality, and image count parameters.
Shows how standard and HD quality affect credit usage.
Describes CDN URL responses for generated images.
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account, API key, and available credits.
Sends prompts and authentication details to the verging.ai service.
Documents generation only; it does not provide local image editing tools.
Examples focus on 1024x1024 output and 1 to 4 images per request.

use_cases.title: Create Concept Art
use_cases.description: Generate quick visual directions for mood boards, prototypes, or campaign ideas.

use_cases.title: Produce Product Mockup Images
use_cases.description: Request consistent image outputs while working inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

use_cases.title: Automate Visual Drafts
use_cases.description: Use documented API parameters to create repeatable image generation workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate One Simple Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single visual asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate one standard quality image of a clean workspace for a productivity app.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several concepts before choosing a direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four standard quality variations of a modern ecommerce hero image with neutral lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Specify Quality and Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Control the style and output cost for a polished asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate two HD images of a futuristic city at sunset in a detailed digital art style.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an API Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user planning repeatable generation through the documented endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me structure a verging.ai image generation request for three 1024x1024 product concept images using GPT-Image-1.

output_examples.input: A request for two HD images of a futuristic cityscape at sunset.
output_examples.output: The response should describe two generated image links hosted on the verging.ai CDN and the credits consumed.

output_examples.input: A request for one standard quality product mockup image.
output_examples.output: The response should provide one generated image URL and note the standard quality credit cost.

output_examples.input: A request that violates the image provider policy.
output_examples.output: The response should report a policy or validation error instead of returning image URLs.

best_practices: Keep API keys in environment variables or secret storage, not in prompts.
Review prompts for sensitive information before sending them to the API.
Start with standard quality before using HD for final assets.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys into shared chats or public examples.
Do not send confidential customer data inside image prompts.
Do not assume generated images are licensed for every commercial use.

faq.question: What service does this skill use?
faq.answer: It documents image generation through the verging.ai API.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. Requests require a verging.ai API key in the Authorization header.

faq.question: Which models are documented?
faq.answer: The skill references DALL-E 3 and GPT-Image-1.

faq.question: Can it generate more than one image?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented n parameter supports 1 to 4 images per request.

faq.question: Where are generated images returned?
faq.answer: The documented response returns CDN-hosted image URLs.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Prompts and API authentication are sent to a third-party service, so users should avoid sensitive data.
